👩‍⚕️ About the Doctor

Meet Dr. Priyanti Thombare

A passionate pediatric dentist dedicated to making every child's dental experience positive and fear-free.

Dr. Priyanti Thombare
B.D.S, M.D.S (Pediatric Dentist)
(Dharmadhikari)
🦷 Pediatric Dental Specialist

Expert Care, Gentle Hands

Dr. Priyanti Thombare is a specialist in pediatric dentistry with over a decade of experience in making dental visits comfortable and enjoyable for children. Her clinic, Sweet Tooth, is built around the philosophy that early dental care shapes a lifetime of healthy smiles.

She brings clinical excellence combined with a warm, child-friendly approach — ensuring kids feel safe and parents feel confident.

  • 🎓
    B.D.S — Bachelor of Dental Surgery VSPM Dental College, Nagpur · 2012
  • 🏅
    M.D.S — Pediatric & Preventive Dentistry Sharad Pawar Dental College · 2016
🦷 Our Services

What We Treat

Comprehensive dental care for kids and families — all under one roof.

Hygiene

Teeth Cleaning & Polishing

Professional scaling and polishing to remove plaque, tartar, and stains — keeping your teeth healthy and bright.

Restorative

Root Canal Treatment

Gentle root canal therapy to save infected or damaged teeth, relieve pain, and restore normal function.

Restorative

Caps & Bridges

Custom dental crowns and bridges to restore damaged or missing teeth, giving you a natural-looking, functional smile.

Surgical

Extractions

Safe and pain-free tooth extractions for severely decayed, damaged, or overcrowded teeth — especially for children.

Pediatric

Space Maintainers

Custom space maintainers to hold the gap after early tooth loss, guiding permanent teeth to erupt in the right position.

Preventive

Preventive Care — Fluorides & Sealants

Fluoride treatments and dental sealants to protect children's teeth from cavities and build long-term oral health habits.

Surgery

Oral Surgery

Minor oral surgical procedures performed with precision and care — from frenectomies to impacted tooth management.

Orthodontics

Orthodontic Treatment

Braces and alignment solutions to straighten teeth and correct bite issues — for children, teens, and adults.

Pain-Free

Treatment Under Laughing Gas

Nitrous oxide sedation makes dental procedures completely relaxed and anxiety-free — especially great for nervous children.

Advanced

Treatment Under General Anesthesia

For complex procedures or highly anxious young patients, we offer safe treatment under general anesthesia in a fully equipped setup.

❓ FAQs

Frequently Asked Questions

Everything parents want to know — answered simply.

🍼

When should I bring my child for their first visit?

Within 6 months of their first tooth, or by their first birthday — whichever comes first. Early visits prevent cavities and build healthy habits.

🪥

Should I brush my child's teeth?

Yes! Start brushing as soon as the first tooth erupts. Use a soft brush with a tiny smear of fluoride toothpaste to protect those first precious teeth.

📡

Are dental X-rays safe for children?

Yes. We use digital X-rays with very low radiation — far less than a chest X-ray — and only take them when clinically necessary.

🧒

How do you handle dental anxiety in children?

We use a gentle "tell-show-do" technique. Our fun, kid-friendly clinic ensures children feel safe and comfortable. Most kids leave smiling!

👨‍👩‍👧

Do you treat adults too?

Absolutely! While we specialise in pediatric dentistry, Sweet Tooth is a full family dental clinic offering care for adults too.

🦷

At what age can my child start orthodontic treatment?

Orthodontic treatment typically starts between ages 10–14. However, an early evaluation around age 7 helps catch issues while the jaw is still growing.

📅

How often should my child visit the dentist?

Every 6 months. Regular check-ups catch problems early, keep teeth clean, and reinforce good brushing and flossing habits from a young age.

💨

What is laughing gas and is it safe for children?

Laughing gas (nitrous oxide) is a safe, mild sedation that helps anxious children relax during treatment. It wears off quickly and has no side effects.
